sn35.63:1.2: Then Venerable Migajāla went to the *Bhagavā* …
sn35.63:1.3: Sitting to one side, Venerable Migajāla said this to the *Bhagavā*:
sn35.63:1.4: “‘One who dwells alone, one who dwells alone,’ it is said, *bhante*.
sn35.63:1.5: To what extent, *bhante*, is one who dwells alone, and to what extent is one who dwells with a companion?”
sn35.63:2.1: “There are, Migajāla, forms [*rūpā*] cognizable by the eye that are wished for, lovely, agreeable, endearing, connected with sensual desire, and enticing.
sn35.63:2.10: then, as he delights in them, welcomes them, and remains holding to them, delight arises.
sn35.63:2.11: When there is delight, there is passion;
sn35.63:2.12: when there is passion, there is bondage.
sn35.63:2.13: A *bhikkhu* bound by the fetter of delight, Migajāla, is called one who dwells with a companion.
sn35.63:2.14: And a *bhikkhu* dwelling in this way, Migajāla, even if he resorts to remote lodgings in the wilderness and forest groves, with little noise, little clamor, secluded from people, hidden from human beings, fit for retreat,
sn35.63:2.15: still he is called one who dwells with a companion.
sn35.63:2.17: Because *taṇhā* is his companion, and it has not been abandoned by him.
sn35.63:2.18: Therefore he is called ‘one who dwells with a companion.’
sn35.63:2.2: If a *bhikkhu* delights in them, welcomes them, and remains holding to them,
sn35.63:2.3: then, as he delights in them, welcomes them, and remains holding to them, delight arises.
sn35.63:2.4: When there is delight, there is passion;
sn35.63:2.5: when there is passion, there is bondage.
sn35.63:2.6: A *bhikkhu* bound by the fetter of delight, Migajāla, is called one who dwells with a companion.
sn35.63:2.8: There are, Migajāla, tastes cognizable by the tongue that are wished for, lovely, agreeable, endearing, connected with sensual desire, and enticing.
sn35.63:2.9: If a *bhikkhu* delights in them, welcomes them, and remains holding to them,
sn35.63:3.1: And there are, Migajāla, forms [*rūpā*] cognizable by the eye that are wished for, lovely, agreeable, endearing, connected with sensual desire, and enticing.
sn35.63:3.10: then, as he does not delight in them, does not welcome them, and does not remain holding to them, delight ceases.
sn35.63:3.11: When there is no delight, there is no passion;
sn35.63:3.12: when there is no passion, there is no bondage.
sn35.63:3.13: A *bhikkhu* separated from the fetter of delight, Migajāla, is called one who dwells alone.
sn35.63:3.14: And a *bhikkhu* dwelling in this way, Migajāla, even if he dwells near a village, crowded with *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhunis*, male lay followers, female lay followers, kings, royal ministers, wanderers of other sects, and disciples of wanderers of other sects,
sn35.63:3.15: still he is called one who dwells alone.
sn35.63:3.17: Because *taṇhā* is his companion, and it has been abandoned by him.
sn35.63:3.18: Therefore he is called ‘one who dwells alone.’”
sn35.63:3.2: If a *bhikkhu* does not delight in them, does not welcome them, and does not remain holding to them,
sn35.63:3.3: then, as he does not delight in them, does not welcome them, and does not remain holding to them, delight ceases.
sn35.63:3.4: When there is no delight, there is no passion;
sn35.63:3.5: when there is no passion, there is no bondage.
sn35.63:3.6: A *bhikkhu* disconnected from the fetter of delight, Migajāla, is called one who dwells alone …
sn35.63:3.7: and there are, Migajāla, tastes cognizable by the tongue …
sn35.63:3.8: and there are, Migajāla, *dhammas* cognizable by the mind that are wished for, lovely, agreeable, endearing, connected with sensual desire, and enticing.
sn35.63:3.9: If a *bhikkhu* does not delight in them, does not welcome them, and does not remain holding to them,
